tools.name: BRICKSTORM
tools.evidence_snippet: BRICKSTORM is a custom Executable and Linkable Format (ELF) Go- or Rust-based backdoor.

yara_rules.name: CISA_251165_02
yara_rules.evidence_snippet: rule CISA_251165_02 : BRICKSTORM backdoor installs_other_components communicates_with_c2 exfiltrates_data

yara_rules.name: CISA_251155_01
yara_rules.evidence_snippet: rule CISA_251155_01 : BRICKSTORM backdoor installs_other_components communicates_with_c2 exfiltrates_data

ttps.evidence_snippet: The web server was inside the organization’s demilitarized zone (DMZ), and cyber actors accessed it through a web shell [T1505.003] present on the server.
The cyber actors used service account credentials [T1078] to move laterally using Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) [T1021.001]
The cyber actors used service account credentials [T1078] to move laterally using Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) [T1021.001]
The cyber actors copied the Active Directory (AD) database (ntds.dit) [T1003.003]
BRICKSTORM initiates by running checks and maintains persistence by using a self-watching function and automatically reinstalls or restarts if disrupted.
The modified init file controls the bootup process [T1037] on VMware vSphere systems and executes BRICKSTORM.
The SOCKS Handler sets up a SOCKS proxy [T1090.001] to route C2 traffic and facilitate lateral movement within the victim network.

ttps.technique: Exfiltration Over Command and Control Channel
ttps.evidence_snippet: BRICKSTORM provides a SOCKS proxy to facilitate tunneling and lateral movement.
The Web Service Handler establishes covert C2 communication by creating a legitimate-appearing web server on the compromised system.
The parent instance modifies the PATH environment variable by appending the copied location’s path [T1574.007].

summary: BRICKSTORM is a custom Executable and Linkable Format (ELF) Go- or Rust-based backdoor used for long-term persistence on victim systems.

timeline.event: Initial access to the web server
timeline.evidence_snippet: The cyber actors accessed a web server on April 11, 2024.

timeline.event: Lateral movement from the web server to a domain controller
timeline.evidence_snippet: On April 12, 2024, the cyber actors moved laterally from the web server to a domain controller within the internal network using RDP and credentials associated with a second service account.
